descriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Red Deer Rustlers 6-4 in Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal game to lead series 2-0.
descriptionLong: Richmond Sockeyes extended their post-season winning streak to 17 consecutive games with a 6-4 victory over Red Deer Rustlers on April 8, 1987, in a Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal at Richmond Arena. Trailing 4-3 with 13 minutes remaining, Richmond scored three goals during a five-minute power play after Rustler defenceman Keith Lightbown was ejected. Coach Orland Kurtenbach's team now leads the best-of-seven series 2-0, with remaining games scheduled in Red Deer.

attribution.headline: Richmond Sockeyes Win Centennial Cup Semifinal Against Red Deer
